overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active and diverse social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, accumulating a total of 3,580 followers. The content predominantly revolves around academic research, publications, grant announcements, and career opportunities in computational social science and disinformation studies. While LinkedIn boasts the largest follower count, Bluesky demonstrates the highest per-post engagement rate, indicating a highly receptive audience there. Overall engagement shows a healthy interest in professional and academic updates.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of new papers, tool releases (e.g., CooRnet v2.0), and general research findings in computational social science and disinformation.
Posts related to hiring (postdocs, PhD students) and new grant awards for research projects.
Updates about upcoming presentations at academic conferences and discussions on methodological approaches.

recommendations: Continue to leverage LinkedIn for major professional announcements like grant awards and hiring, as it reaches the largest and most professional audience, yielding significant likes and comments.

Capitalize on Bluesky's high engagement for immediate announcements of new publications and research/PhD opportunities. Consider linking back to Bluesky posts from other platforms where appropriate for cross-promotion.
Explore creating more interactive content or Q&A sessions on Mastodon and Bluesky to further boost discussion, given the strong engagement on academic discourse observed.
Analyze optimal posting times for each platform to maximize reach, particularly on Bluesky where engagement per post is highest, and maintain a consistent frequency of updates across all profiles.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits the highest average per-post engagement rate (6.14%), indicating a very active and receptive audience. Content announcing new publications and research/PhD opportunities performs exceptionally well, often leading to strong repost activity and demonstrating a highly engaged academic community.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon, while having the smallest follower count, generates steady engagement for technical updates (like CooRnet v2.0) and academic discussions. It serves as a valuable platform for connecting with a more niche, academically-focused audience who actively engage with detailed research news.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn boasts the largest follower base (1,850 followers) and is highly effective for broadcasting significant professional news such as grant awards and hiring announcements. Posts on this platform tend to generate a high volume of likes and comments, reinforcing its role as a primary channel for professional networking and key announcements.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
